act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act 2013 (POSH)
text: The POSH Act protects women from sexual harassment at any workplace — offices, factories, hospitals, schools, universities, shops, hotels, NGOs, domestic work, or even online workplaces. Sexual harassment includes: unwelcome physical contact or advances; demand or request for sexual favours; sexually coloured remarks; showing pornography; any other unwelcome physical, verbal or non-verbal conduct of a sexual nature. Extended definition includes quid pro quo (give me this or I will harm your career) and hostile work environment harassment.

act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act 2013 (POSH)
text: A woman who has been sexually harassed can file a written complaint with the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) within three months of the incident (extendable by another three months). If the organisation has less than 10 employees, complaint goes to the Local Complaints Committee (LCC) at the district level. The ICC must complete inquiry within 60 days. If found guilty, action includes: written apology, warning, demotion, withholding of promotion, termination, or payment of compensation. The identity of the complainant is confidential.

act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act 2013 (POSH)
text: Every employer shall: (1) Provide a safe working environment; (2) Display information about the POSH Act and ICC conspicuously; (3) Organise workshops and awareness programmes; (4) Provide assistance to women if they choose to file a police complaint; (5) Treat sexual harassment as a misconduct and initiate action under the service rules; (6) Submit annual report to the district officer on the number of cases filed. Employers with 10 or more employees MUST constitute an ICC. Failure to do so attracts fine of Rs 50,000 (doubled for repeat offence) and cancellation of business licence.

ESI provides the following benefits to insured employees: (1) Medical benefit — full medical care for employee and family; (2) Sickness benefit — 70% of wages during certified sickness up to 91 days; (3) Maternity benefit — full wages for 26 weeks; (4) Disablement benefit — 90% of wages for temporary/permanent disability; (5) Dependants benefit — 90% of wages to dependants on employee's death due to employment injury; (6) Funeral expenses — Rs 15,000. ESI applies to all factories and establishments with 10 or more employees where wages do not exceed Rs 21,000 per month.

act_name: Employees Provident Fund Act 1952 — EPF Withdrawal Rules
text: EPF (Provident Fund) can be withdrawn in these situations: (1) Retirement after 58 years — full withdrawal; (2) Unemployment for 2+ months — can withdraw 75% after 1 month; (3) Medical emergency — up to 6 months wages for self or family; (4) Marriage — up to 50% of employee's share after 7 years service; (5) Education — up to 50% after 7 years; (6) Home purchase or construction — up to 90% after 5 years; (7) Home loan repayment — up to 90% after 10 years. EPF withdrawal is done online through the EPFO unified portal. Form 31 for partial withdrawal, Form 19 for final settlement. EPFO helpline: 1800-118-005.
